# Git Performance Test
# Tests performance with aggressive timeouts

# Build the git server binary
go build -o mcp-git-server .

# Create a test git repository
mkdir test_git_repo
cd test_git_repo
git init
git config user.name "Test User"
git config user.email "test@example.com"
echo "# Test Repo" > README.md
git add README.md
git commit -m "Initial commit"

# Test with aggressive timeout (3 seconds)
timeout 3s exec ../mcp-git-server . <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{"roots":{"listChanged":true},"sampling":{}}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/list","params":{},"id":2}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"git_status","arguments":{"repository":"."}},"id":3}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"git_log","arguments":{"repository":".","max_count":1}},"id":4}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"git_branches","arguments":{"repository":"."}},"id":5}
EOF

# Verify operations complete within timeout
stdout '"name":"git_status"'
stdout 'Initial commit'
stdout 'main'

# Test rapid sequential git operations
timeout 5s exec ../mcp-git-server . <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{"roots":{"listChanged":true},"sampling":{}}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"git_status","arguments":{"repository":"."}},"id":3}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"git_status","arguments":{"repository":"."}},"id":4}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"git_status","arguments":{"repository":"."}},"id":5}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"git_log","arguments":{"repository":".","max_count":1}},"id":6}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"git_branches","arguments":{"repository":"."}},"id":7}
EOF

# Verify rapid calls are handled correctly
stdout 'main'

# Test with multiple file operations (should be fast)
echo "File 1" > file1.txt
echo "File 2" > file2.txt
echo "File 3" > file3.txt

timeout 4s exec ../mcp-git-server . <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{"roots":{"listChanged":true},"sampling":{}}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"git_add","arguments":{"repository":".","files":["file1.txt","file2.txt","file3.txt"]}},"id":3}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"git_commit","arguments":{"repository":".","message":"Add multiple files"}},"id":4}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"git_status","arguments":{"repository":"."}},"id":5}
EOF

# Verify multiple file operations are handled quickly
stdout 'Successfully added files'
stdout 'Add multiple files'

# Cleanup
cd ..
rm -rf test_git_repo
